channel.title: Milk Drinks on Barista At Home
channel.description: Recent content in Milk Drinks on Barista At Home

item.title: Magic Coffee Recipe: Melbourne's Secret-Menu Double Ristretto Drink
item.description: How to make a magic coffee — Melbourne's signature 5 oz drink: 2 ristretto shots topped with steamed milk in a small glass. Includes recipe, magic vs flat white vs piccolo comparison, and why baristas love it.

item.title: What Is a Piccolo Coffee? The Australian Mini-Latte Explained (Piccolo vs Cortado vs Macchiato)
item.description: A piccolo is a 4 oz Australian espresso drink — one ristretto shot topped with steamed milk in a small glass. Includes piccolo vs cortado vs macchiato comparison, ratios, recipe, and why baristas love it.

item.title: Macchiato vs. Cappuccino: What's the Difference?
item.description: A macchiato is espresso with a small dollop of foam (1–2 oz). A cappuccino is a 5–6 oz drink with equal parts espresso, steamed milk, and thick foam. Learn the key differences in size, taste, and milk ratio.

item.title: What Is a Cappuccino? Definition, Ratio, and Types Explained
item.description: A cappuccino is an espresso drink made with equal thirds of espresso, steamed milk, and dense milk foam. Learn what separates a cappuccino from a latte, macchiato, and flat white.

item.title: Cortado vs Latte: What's the Difference?
item.description: A cortado uses equal parts espresso and steamed milk (1:1). A latte uses 5–6x more milk than espresso. Here's the full comparison including size, strength, foam, and when to order each.

item.title: Flat White vs Cappuccino: What's the Difference?
item.description: Flat white and cappuccino both use espresso and milk, but they differ in size, milk texture, foam depth, and strength. Here's exactly how they compare.

item.title: What Is a Latte Macchiato? (And How to Make One at Home)
item.description: A latte macchiato is milk 'stained' with espresso — the opposite layering of a regular latte. Here's what makes it different, how to make it, and how it compares to a latte and cappuccino.

item.title: Caramel Macchiato Recipe: How to Make It at Home
item.description: A caramel macchiato is espresso layered over vanilla-steamed milk and finished with caramel drizzle. Learn how to make the classic — hot or iced — at home, better than Starbucks.

item.title: Flat White vs Latte: What's the Difference?
item.description: A flat white is smaller (5-6 oz) with a stronger espresso flavor and velvety microfoam. A latte is larger (8-12 oz) and creamier. Here's exactly how they differ.

item.title: Macchiato vs Latte: What's the Difference? (All Three Types Explained)
item.description: A macchiato is a tiny 2–3 oz espresso stained with a dot of foam. A latte is 8–12 oz with lots of steamed milk. Here's how traditional macchiatos, latte macchiatos, and lattes actually differ.

item.title: Cappuccino vs Latte: What's the Difference?
item.description: Cappuccino has thick foam and a stronger espresso flavor. Latte is larger and creamier with a thin layer of microfoam. Learn the differences in ratio, texture, taste, and how to make each.

item.title: What Is a Macchiato? The Complete Guide to Every Type
item.description: A macchiato is an espresso 'stained' with a small amount of milk. Learn what makes each type different — espresso macchiato, latte macchiato, and the Starbucks version.

item.title: Cappuccino Recipe: How to Make a Cappuccino at Home
item.description: Learn how to make a cappuccino at home — the classic ratio, steaming technique, and tips for getting perfect microfoam without a professional machine.

item.title: Latte Recipe: How to Make a Latte at Home
item.description: Make a café-quality latte at home — the exact recipe, milk steaming technique, and tips for getting smooth microfoam every time.
